mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, mechanically compressing the present value of future corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower corporate profit margins and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate robust aggregate demand expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macro environment, a sharp drop in industrial production triggers supply-side inflation expectations and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO declines below 95 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with rising corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional capital flows into expanding economic activity.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in GDP-growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 235, persistent inflation expectations force long-duration equity valuations lower, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates reprice.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stagnant total nonfarm payroll growth signals weakening labor demand, which mechanically reduces consumer spending and corporate cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in deteriorating labor markets are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit-expansion regime, rapid month-over-month growth in consumer credit balances fuels immediate retail sales momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative when RSAFS increases by more than 50,000 million dollars month-over-month for three consecutive months.
